methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Panthalassa is a Series A climate and energy company founded by Garth Sheldon-Coulson. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 3 private companies at the same stage and sector: Graphyte ($200M), Fourth Power ($150M), and Steady Energy ($110M). The broader comparable universe includes 8 private peers and 1 public company (LNZA), with additional reference to later-stage private companies including TAE Technologies and Redwood Materials.

The peer-median method is the applicable triangulation approach given available data. The median valuation across the three same-stage, same-sector peers is $150M, with a stated range of $110M to $200M. This method directly reflects current market pricing for climate and energy companies at Series A stage.

The triangulated valuation range implied by this analysis is $128M to $172M, centered on a median of $150M. This range reflects the bounds observed in the comparable set with a modest haircut at the low end. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the three-company peer set, the extent to which Panthalassa's specific technology, market position, or traction diverges from peers, and the relevance of public market comparables. Without access to revenue multiples, growth rates, or technology differentiation metrics, the analysis relies primarily on stage and sector matching.
